jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager
jobs.description: Fixed term contract for 12 months Join our rapidly growing business in a role as Digital Marketing Manager (FTC) focusing primarily on the digital marketing and paid digital strategies to support B2B’s commercial growth, as well as managing the apetito.co.uk website strategy. As a digital marketing specialist, your role will be pivotal in enhancing our online presence. Reporting to the Head of Proposition & Marketing, you will play a key role in contributing to the company’s ambitious growth targets and driving our online brand visibility. This is a fantastic opportunity for a collaborative, analytical and creative marketeer to work within a supportive, experienced and passionate team. This role is based in Trowbridge and you will be required to work on-site at least 3 days per week.

jobs.title: Senior PR Executive
jobs.description: As Senior PR Executive, you will play a key role in delivering high-quality, impactful PR activity that strengthens apetito’s reputation across our priority markets. You will need a high degree of autonomy and judgement, and be trusted to lead the delivery of high-profile PR activity under the direction of the PR Manager. Sitting within the central Brand & Communications team, you will work closely with colleagues across the business and external partners to bring our stories to life, ensuring our voice is credible, consistent and values-led. You will also act as a role model within the PR team, helping to maintain high standards and support the development of others. Most importantly, you will help share how apetito and Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference to customers, communities and colleagues every single day. At apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods, we are on an ambitious journey. With bold growth targets ahead, how we tell our story and protect our reputation will play a critical role in helping us succeed. We are looking for someone with experience in PR and media relations, able to craft compelling stories as well as demonstrate the commercial impact of PR delivery. This role is based in our Trowbridge head office and you will need to be on site at least 3 days per week.

jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 40 hours per week, any 5 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour(£13.45 per hour from 1 May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Technical Manager
jobs.description: Technical Manager – Be at the Heart of a Major New Investment Join apetito at a transformational moment in our journey. Having exceeded £260m turnover, we are now investing over £100m in building a state‑of‑the‑art manufacturing facility —a major milestone that will support our continued growth and innovation in food manufacturing. As our new Technical Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role not only in leading food safety and quality during the operational phase, but also in shaping the future of the site as it comes to life. This includes valuable involvement throughout the installation and commissioning of the new facility—an opportunity few roles can offer. What You’ll Be Doing While construction is underway, you’ll be based in our existing kitchen, located just a short walk from the new site. Reporting to the Group Technical Manager, you will: Work closely with the current Site Technical Manager to support the team with a strong focus on systems, governance, and compliance Build a deep understanding of our complex operation—managing hundreds of ingredients and materials that go into producing hundreds of delicious, nutritious recipes Begin partnering with the project team as the new build progresses, ensuring that food safety and quality standards are fully embedded throughout installation and commissioning Recruit, develop, and inspire your new team, building strong relationships across the site Design and implement robust quality systems to support the new kitchen’s launch Ensure we achieve food registration and secure an AA grade against the BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety at the earliest opportunity

jobs.title: Customer Service Advisor
jobs.description: This role is working 30 hours per week, any 4 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.description: Shape the future of healthcare design with HGA! Project Architect – Healthcare in Sacramento, San Francisco, Santa Monica or San Diego, CA HGA, a leading architectural, engineering, and planning firm, is seeking a talented and driven Project Architect to join our growing healthcare practice group in Northern and Southern California. We’re looking for someone with strong communication, presentation, time management, and interpersonal skills who thrives in a collaborative environment and is passionate about advancing healthcare design. In this role, you’ll work closely with Senior Designers, Project Managers, and other Project Architects to develop innovative and technically sound design solutions. You’ll lead the creation of detailed documentation while maintaining our firm’s high standards for design quality and client service. What You’ll Do: Lead the development of construction documents and ensure design intent is accurately carried through. Translate complex project goals into creative and functional design solutions. Collaborate across disciplines to coordinate architectural and engineering systems. Participate in client meetings and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders. Ensure project milestones and deadlines are met with attention to detail and quality. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful healthcare projects that improve patient outcomes and shape the future of care in Calfornia. In this role you will: Design and u nderstand and contribute to the project's design philosophy. Provide major input in developing design and building performance strategies and convey these effectively to team members. Lead project teams to develop complete document sets based on the project phase. Provide technical design input and solutions to project challenges, supporting the overall design concept. Oversee technical design efforts, detailed design development, project coordination, and quality control during all project phases, collaborating closely with the Senior Designer. Execute applicable agency review analysis (accessibility, zoning, life-safety, etc.). Actively collaborate with and coordinate the work of consultants and knowledge resources to ensure project deliverables are coordinated, integrated, and support the design concept and performance goals. Set an example for team effort through the spirit of cooperation while directing, organizing, and mentoring junior staff. C ontribute to the preparation of design documents and lead the detailed development of construction documents during all project phases. Ensure complete, accurate, and coordinated project documentation. Oversee and perform construction administration duties, including reviewing shop drawings, material samples, RFI’s, RFP’s, and change orders for conformance with design. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Architecture California Architectural Licensure required LEED accreditation preferred 10+ years of experience, including work on small to large-scale projects. Understanding of the healthcare industry, especially in areas of clinical innovation, operational improvement, planning, and strategic positioning for complex hospital systems Deep understanding of healthcare programming, codes, and technical detailing Strong leadership and collaboration skills in team environments Proficiency in Revit required; experience with AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape, and other graphic tools desired. Expertise with project management software and Microsoft Office Suite products needed Excellent communication, presentation, and time management skills Experience with Lean and Evidence-based design is a plus. Experience with alternative delivery methods (IPD, Design Build, IFOA) preferred Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $108,200 - $130,000 depending on location (SAC, SF, LA or SD), qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume and portfolio for consideration.

We are looking for a talented, design-sensitive, and ambitious Project Manager to join our California healthcare architecture teams and help shape the future of healthcare design. In this role, you will be an integral member of our project teams, contributing at every stage of the design process. Our practice encompasses a broad array of project types and scales, so versatility and adaptability to various collaborative design team settings are essential. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful projects that improve and shape the future architectural design. In this role you will: Collaborate with Principal in evaluation of project requirements and help to negotiate an acceptable owner-architect agreement including the fee, scope, and time schedule. Demonstrate task-focused mastery of project scope, schedule, budget and profitability. Complete projects on schedule, within budget and with maximum client satisfaction. Manage a program of multiple projects and associated client relationships effectively. Negotiate critical issues with Owner and Contractor as well as Architects, Engineers and Specialty Consultants within HGA and of other organizations and firms. Supervise the production of architectural construction documents for multiple small renovation projects. Lead a full-service architectural and engineering team, using past experience and technical knowledge to efficiently deliver quality documents, specifications, reports and cost estimates, keeping in mind schedules/budgets and provides coaching/communication as appropriate with team leaders and/or team members who are responsible for work flow/timelines, etc. Develop discipline cost and work-hour budgets for each project and monitor project costs, completion status against budget schedules/timelines. Oversee all correspondence and pertinent documents, making sure they are accurate; sign or obtain signatures and seek legal counsel as appropriate. Coordinate review and evaluation of contractor bids and work with Principal so that award recommendation can be submitted to owner. Provide day-to-day leadership in the absence of and as delegated by the Principal and/or Practice Group Leader keeping lines of communication open with all office management leaders and assist with preparation of client billings prior to submittal for signature. Collaborate with Practice Group Leader as requested in the marketing of projects including development of proposals and possible participation in client interviews. jobs.title: RTR Manager
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional’s and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. ABBOTT IRELAND Abbott serves the Irish market with a diverse range of health care products including diagnostics, medical devices and nutritional products. In Ireland, Abbott employs almost 6,000 people across ten sites. We have six manufacturing facilities located in Clonmel, Cootehill, Donegal, Kilkenny, Longford and Sligo and a third-party manufacturing management operation in Sligo. Abbott has commercial, support operations and shared services in Dublin and Galway. We have been operating in Ireland since 1946. We currently have a vacancy for a Manager within our Record to Report section of the Shared Services team, supporting the Netherlands market. The successful candidate will join the Shared Services Centre based either in Cherrywood Business Park, Loughlinstown, Co Dublin or Ballybrit Business Park in Galway KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Leads an accounting team, performing general accounting processes from the point of transaction through to financial close and periodic external reporting (monthly, quarterly, and annually) in support of corporate, country/legal entity and regional controllers Responsible for Month end close process, close governance, associated corporate reporting, and ensuring prompt response to Internal/ External Audit requests for in scope countries Support the advancement of the Target Operating Model (TOM) by identifying additional activities to be transitioned to the outsource partner(s), while continuing to transition more complex activities into the CFS EMEA Hub Report on agreed performance measures, KPIs, and goal setting processes to support Shared Services Support and guide the internal and external teams to identify, implement and maintain effective controls for the finance process activities performed to ensure ongoing data and financial integrity for Abbott Support the continued development of team members utilizing Abbott’s training and development tools Manage the delivery of accounting activities provided by our 3rd party BPO provider for in scope countries Lead the team on ad hoc and special projects assignments, including system migrations and embed a continuous improvement mindset within the team Sustain Service Agreement delivery standards with the internal customers and 3rd party outsource service providers. KEY REQUIREMENTS : Accounting Qualification required (ACCA/CIMA/ACA/CPA) BSc in Accounting or Finance preferred 5+ years relevant experience in a complex global environment – preferably within Shared Services servicing a multi-currency, US GAAP based financial close 3-5 years team lead experience, mentoring and developing teams Experience working with working with 3rd party service providers essential Highly Motivated with strong communication, technical, analytical, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work and collaborate with others, building/leveraging relationships across functions and organizations to achieve business results. Continuous improvement mindset and proven track record in delivering projects Language Skills:  English required, other languages desirable Preferred Experience Audit experience preferred Experience in development, implementation and optimisation of Hybrid Target Operation Models to meet changing business needs The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. jobs.description: Plumbing Designer - Washington, DC Join HGA—an award-winning architecture, engineering, interior design, and planning firm—where your technical skills and creative thinking can make a national impact. We’re seeking a Plumbing Designer to join our growing Mechanical team. At HGA, you’ll collaborate with a multidisciplinary group of architects, engineers, and designers on meaningful projects spanning healthcare, science & technology, cultural institutions, corporate campuses, and infrastructure . Every project is an opportunity to shape the built environment using cutting-edge tools and innovative thinking . What You'll Do: Design plumbing systems including domestic water, sanitary waste/vent, medical gas, storm drainage, and specialty systems. Collaborate with team members across disciplines from concept through construction. Contribute to technical documentation, specifications, and code compliance. Leverage BIM/Revit and other digital tools to develop coordinated designs. If you're ready to bring your passion to impactful, high-performance designs that shape communities nationwide—we want to hear from you. In this role you will: Be responsible for plumbing design including calculating building loads for all plumbing systems, select equipment, layout piping, create and edit project details, and write and edit project engineering specifications. Participate in design review meetings and workshops. Support a variety of complex design challenges including conflicting Owner and project requirements, application of different system types, or challenging field conditions. Research design options and document findings for the project team; Assure drawings and designs are in accordance with local codes and design standards. Assist with coordination with architectural and other engineering disciplines as applicable. You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in architectural engineering, mechanical engineering or related field desired. Up to 2 years of consulting engineering experience with emphasis on plumbing design for healthcare, commercial, municipal, institutional and government facilities. Knowledge of applicable codes and regulations, standard engineering practices, techniques, and procedures. Excellent problem-solving skills. Ability to work on several projects simultaneously and prioritize work. Excellent communication skills. Positive attitude, team player with a collaborative approach. Interest in professional growth and mentoring. Revit, BIM, and Microsoft Office proficiency required. Professional Engineer or Certification recognized by a state licensing board desired. Knowledge in HVAC systems and building controls preferred, but not required. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted . Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $42.42/hr (new graduate rate) - $46.42/hr depending on qualifications and experience . Additional Information: HGA is a place where you can do your best work. We believe that enduring, impactful design results from deep insight into the people and passions that animate each unique environment. We value empathy, are fueled by curiosity, and embrace the hard work that leads to innovation. Equally important is our commitment to creating a culture of inclusivity and diversity where we encourage your originality, respect the need for flexibility and work-life balance, and empower your desired career growth. HGA is a national award-winning interdisciplinary design firm rooted in architecture and engineering. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.description: Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist MemorialCare Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach MemorialCare Medical Group Base Salary Range*: $300,000 to $310,000 *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position. Your Total Rewards would include a base salary plus performance-based incentives and other valuable benefits. Our compensation models utilize fair market value data specific to your specialty. Memorial Care Medical Group (MCMG) is looking for a Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist for our dedicated Pediatric Cardiothoracic Intensive Care Unit (CTICU) at Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach (MCWHLB). The new state of the art 8-bed CTICU and an additional 6-bed stepdown unit are part of our Heart Institute’s rapid growth to serve the needs of the southern California community. Candidates with strong clinical and teaching skills are encouraged to apply. The candidate should be board-certified in Pediatric Critical Care plus advanced fellowship training in Pediatric Cardiac Critical Care or a record of excellence in the clinical care of congenital cardiac patients. The successful candidate will work closely with the CTICU Medical Director Dr. Constantinos Chrysostomou and a multidisciplinary team of pediatric cardiac intensivists, cardiac surgeons, cardiologists, dedicated CTICU nurses and other specialists to further develop a state-of-the-art clinical program. Successful candidates should have (or be eligible for) an unrestricted California medical license. As part of the Memorial Care Medical Group, you will be given the tools you need to build the career you want, and the flexibility to adapt as your personal needs and professional interests change. We invite you to grow with us and help shape the future of this community. Robust compensation and benefits package are provided. Memorial Care Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach, is a private, not for profit children’s hospital first opened in 1970. It is a 324 bed children’s hospital that treats more than 8,000 children each year and has become a regional pediatric destination for more than 84,000 children. It has an active perinatal and maternal fetal medicine service with over 7000 deliveries annually to the adjacent birth center and one of the largest Neonatal Intensive Care units (NICU) in the area with over 90 beds. The hospital serves as the major referral center for the over 500,000 people of Long Beach, but reaches an additional 2 million people in the immediate vicinity. The hospital has an academic affiliation with the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) and the University of California Irvine (UCI) to which the successful candidate is eligible to apply for a faculty position. Financial & Benefits • Employed position with MemorialCare Medical Group • Competitive salary with incentive compensation • Eligible to be considered for Shareholder/Partner status with MCMG after two years of full-time employment • Full and comprehensive benefits for Physician and family • 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching • 529 College Savings Plan About Long Beach and Southern California Located on the coast of the Pacific Ocean south of Los Angeles and just west of Orange County, Long Beach is the sixth largest city in the State of California. Offering all the world class amenities of a large metropolitan city, coupled with its strong sense of community and pride, Long Beach is one of the most vibrant communities in the country. The city has been referred to as the "Most Diverse City" in the country by USA Today and one of the "Best Communities for Young People" two years in a row. With its ideal location in Southern California, year-round comfortable climate, healthy business environment and far ranging cultural pursuits, the city is alive with activity. Long Beach is home to an abundance of cultural and recreational options. Expansive beaches, three marinas, five golf courses, the Aquarium of the Pacific, the Queen Mary and the annual Grand Prix of Long Beach serve to draw 6.5 million visitors a year. Long Beach has easy access to all the amenities and attractions of Los Angeles, Orange, and San Bernardino counties, including professional sporting events, music festivals, gourmet dining, film and television studios, Disneyland, and snow skiing in the local mountains.

jobs.title: Physician, Aortic Surgery Fellow
jobs.description: Base Salary: $120,000 The MemorialCare Long Beach Heart and Vascular Institute is offering a non-ACGME accredited Advanced Aortic Surgery Training program with an anticipated start date of Summer 2026. Employment through MemorialCare Medical Group will end by July 31, 2027. We will consider candidates who are board-eligible or board-certified by the American Board of Thoracic Surgery. The fellowship will have exposure to advanced and complex open, hybrid, and endovascular aortic surgery, TAVR and minimal-invasive atrial fibrillation procedures. Basic and advanced catheter and wire skills training will be offered during the year. Furthermore, there is an opportunity to participate in minimal-invasive heart valve surgery such as mini right thoracotomy AVR or MVR operations. MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center is one of the largest hospitals on the West Coast, with affiliations with the University of California Irvine and the University of California Los Angeles. The Comprehensive Aortic program includes ten vascular and cardiovascular surgeons enhanced by access to the best medical technology, a state-of-the-art hybrid room, and a dedicated patient transfer program. The faculty includes pioneers in aortic and cardiovascular surgery including Dr. White, Dr. Khoynezhad, Dr. Abolhoda, Dr. Chiu, and Dr. Sakwa. This fellowship is a well-structured, mentored program that will involve a diversified experience. In addition to the operations and procedures, the fellow will participate in a research program that encompasses industry-sponsored multi-center trials, physician-sponsored investigational device exemption, and outcomes clinical studies. A publication in peer-reviewed journal and presentation at a national meeting is sponsored and a requirement of the fellowship. For the cardiovascular surgeon this program will include: Ascending aortic and hemi-arch surgery Aortic root replacement surgery including David/Yacoub Concomitant MAZE procedures (concomitant and left thoracotomy) Minimal-invasive AF procedures (total thoracoscopic, sub-xiphoid, hybrid) Descending and thoracoabdominal aortic replacement Aortic arch reconstructions including hybrid Advanced TEVAR Branched arch and thoracoabdominal aortic TEVAR Percutaneous ascending aortic TEVAR EVAR TAVR Minimally invasive mitral, aortic, and tricuspid valve surgery
